anchored_threads.thread_name: Sāṅkhya / first-principles knowledge (verses 2.11-2.30). What survives change; what is configuration vs. essence; the imperishable that is the substrate of action.
anchored_threads.anchor_evidence: The verse closes the sāṅkhya block by universalising the imperishable thesis (the dehī inviolable in all bodies → the customer-need inviolable in every implementation) and naming the closing tasmāt as the bridge to action.

anchored_threads.thread_name: Karma Yoga / disciplined action (verses 2.31-2.53). How to act once one knows the substrate. Includes the famous 2.47.
anchored_threads.anchor_evidence: The engineering layer explicitly names the bridge — 'After this verse, the chapter pivots to duty' — and concretizes the next-action move (migration ticket, runbook, successor merge), positioning 2.30 as the hand-off into the karma-yoga block.

progression_issue.explanation: The verse is the structural pivot from sāṅkhya (2.11-2.30) to karma yoga (2.31-2.53); the engineering layer respects this position by recapitulating the prior block and foreshadowing the next. This is the cleanest progression-fit in the block.
